title: Hardening pass: PROJECT.md persistence from TASK-011 review
description:
Follow-up polish for `src/project-md.js` and its in-house YAML frontmatter parser. None of these block correctness today (the wizard's own data path doesn't trip any of them), but TASK-013 (agent generator) will be the first non-wizard caller of `writeProjectMd` and several of these issues become reachable then.

1. **Comma-in-array-item silent corruption (MEDIUM from TASK-011 review).** `tags: ['hello, world', 'plain']` round-trips as `['hello', 'world', 'plain']`. The writer emits inline arrays as `key: [item1, item2, item3]` and the reader splits on `,` without escape handling. Either escape commas in array items (e.g. percent-encode or backslash-escape) on write and unescape on read, OR reject commas at write time with a clear error and document the constraint in the module docstring. Either fix must keep round-trip lossless for all wizard-generated inputs.

2. **`coerceFrontmatterScalar` integer-string coercion (LOW).** Today `schema_version: 1` benefits, but a future frontmatter field that legitimately needs string `"1"` (e.g. a versioned numeric tag) will silently become Number 1. Gate coercion on the schema's declared type — parse the schema once at module load and coerce only fields with `type: "integer"` or `type: "number"`.

3. **Stack-value coercion asymmetry (LOW).** Stack values like `port: '8080'` round-trip as strings (correct for unknown keys), but the asymmetry vs. frontmatter scalars is undocumented. Add an inline comment in `parseStackValue` explaining the deliberate divergence so a future contributor doesn't "fix" it.

4. **Silent default-empty-string fallback (LOW).** `writeProjectMd` will happily emit `name: \ntype: \n` if `project_name`/`project_type` are absent, then the downstream ajv validation rejects. Throw at write-time with `'missing required answer: <id>'` so bugs surface at the point of error.

5. **Misleading colon comment (NIT).** `src/project-md.js:107` reads "no quoting (values may not contain ':')". Verified that values CAN contain `:` in BODY/Stack sections. Reword to clarify the constraint is frontmatter-only.

Reference: full reviewer report is summarized in the TASK-011 closing comment (2026-05-26T02:00:00Z).
acceptance_criteria: `writeProjectMd` and `readProjectMd` round-trip lossless for array items containing commas — either via escape/unescape or via a write-time throw with a clear error.
`coerceFrontmatterScalar` consults `state/PROJECT.schema.json` for the field's declared type and only coerces numeric strings when the schema says the field is integer or number.
`parseStackValue` carries an inline comment naming the deliberate string-only handling for unknown keys.
`writeProjectMd` throws at write-time when required frontmatter answers (`project_name`, `project_type`) are missing or empty; error message names the missing field.
Comment at `src/project-md.js:107` (or wherever the colon constraint lives after this ticket) accurately describes that the constraint is frontmatter-only.

comments.author: orchestrator
comments.body: Created from the TASK-011 reviewer audit (verdict APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOWUPS, 1 MEDIUM + 4 LOWs concerning src/project-md.js). All five rolled into this single ticket because they touch the same module and most share the same fix profile (schema-aware coercion + clearer error surfaces). Take BEFORE TASK-013 lands since TASK-013 will be the first non-wizard caller of writeProjectMd.
